description: Reviewed residual legacy-term hits after the additive PM collapse call-site migration. Production entries are exact file paths; broad runtime globs are intentionally avoided so new legacy files fail the enforcement test.

allowed.reason: Architecture, PRD, and audit documents describe old-to-new mappings and historical contracts.
Planning context may mention legacy terms while tracking migration state.
Tests seed or assert legacy compatibility during the additive transition; runtime behavior is covered by focused PM-native tests.
The v0.0.2 catalog overlay records source attachment legacy redirects while preserving dispatch compatibility during the additive transition.
Migrations preserve and backfill legacy schema names until the destructive cleanup bead lands.
ReportFilter still accepts legacy instrument/venue filters while PM-native market filters are phased in.
market.bind intentionally creates/returns legacy relational prerequisites during the additive PM collapse so public clients need no hidden venue/instrument setup tools.
external_receipts retains a legitimate non-executing public/local link to instruments while the legacy schema remains present.
paper_fills retains a legitimate local non-executing paper-fill linkage to instruments while the legacy schema remains present.
reconciliation reports compare local legacy position projections against imported evidence while the legacy schema remains present; this is read-only, non-executing diagnostic linkage.
pretrade_intent.record/list retain optional legacy local audit links for compatibility while publicly centering market-scoped non-executing intent packets.
approval.record/list/report retain optional legacy local instrument links for compatibility while storing non-executing approval/waiver audit evidence.
Existing event semantic keys remain stable for replay/idempotency until replacement event versions are introduced.
Import/export code must classify historical journal events during additive transition.
Typed models mirror legacy rows that remain present until destructive cleanup.
Typed models mirror legacy rows that remain present until destructive cleanup.
Playbook predicate metadata and source-attachment predicates are legacy rule surfaces outside this additive PM collapse slice.
Position projections remain instrument-scoped and are outside PM forecast/source collapse.
Read-model rows still expose legacy local ledger IDs during the additive transition.
Read-model rows still expose legacy local ledger IDs during the additive transition.
